summary: Tight Scarlet>Fire, epic drum jam, solid second set
blurb: The show delivers a tight Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain transition and an expansive second‑set sequence featuring Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World, a powerful Egyptian‑beat drum jam and a marathon Black Peter. Jerry’s guitar wails despite vocal strain while Phil anchors the groove; overall performance is engaging throughout.
review: On January 17, 1979 at the Veterans Memorial Coliseum the Dead rolled out a classic late‑70s set that still feels fresh. The first set swings through Shakedown Street and a handful of tight numbers before handing the reins to a second set that truly shines: Scarlet Begonias segues flawlessly into Fire on the Mountain, then rolls straight into an inspired Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World stretch. A standout Egyptian‑beat drum jam erupts from Mickey and Billy, launching a soaring Not Fade Away that feeds into a marathon Black Peter before the band closes with Around & Around and Good Lovin’. Jerry’s voice may have been ragged, but his lead guitar is on fire – shredding through Scarlet>Fire and laying down searing solos on Black Peter. Phil’s bass is deep and well‑balanced, driving both the groove and the drum jam, while Bob’s vocals sit perfectly atop the Scarlet>Fire climax. Keith’s piano draws mixed reactions, yet the overall musicality holds together with tight transitions and generous improvisational peaks. For anyone looking to hear a solid 1979 snapshot of the Dead’s chemistry, this show is a must‑listen.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Four recordings are clearly positive, highlighting the Scarlet>Fire transition, the expansive Estimated Prophet → Eyes of the World jam, and a powerful Egyptian‑beat drum jam. Two mixed reviews still praise key moments, while only one negative comment focuses on recording flaws rather than performance. The consensus across sources is strongly favorable, warranting a solid positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Decent soundboard clarity captures the full show, includes all standout songs, and despite occasional tape hiccups offers the most complete and reliable listening experience.

key_highlights: Scarlet Begonias → Fire on the Mountain segue is tight and vibrant
Epic second‑set flow with Estimated Prophet into Eyes of the World
Powerful Egyptian‑beat drum jam that launches Not Fade Away
Marathon Black Peter performance despite some tape dip

band_performance.Jerry: Voice is ragged but his guitar wails; shredding solos on Scarlet>Fire and Black Peter earn high praise.
band_performance.Phil: Bass is deep, high‑end and well balanced; drives the groove and initiates the drum jam.
band_performance.Bob: Vocals sit perfectly, especially during the Scarlet>Fire climax; generally solid throughout.
band_performance.Mickey: Drums shine in the Egyptian‑beat jam, providing a powerful launch into the second‑set improvisations.
band_performance.Billy: Strong drum work that underpins the long jams and fuels the Not Fade Away transition.
